plant.description: Unique History: A highly sacred 'Jivaniya' (life-promoting) plant mentioned as the wild, uncultivated equivalent of Masha (Black gram). Regarded as essential for reviving life force ('Ayush'). Physical Characteristics: A highly variable, slender, twining perennial herb with appressed hairs on the stem. Leaves are trifoliate, resembling Masha but smaller. Flowers are small, pinkish/purplish to white. The pods are slender, curved, and contain brownish-black rectangular seeds. Found wild throughout Indian plains. Specific Chemical Compounds: Contains Fraxidin, Coumarins, and specific Teramnus proteins. These compounds act as deep neuromuscular sedatives and tissue regenerators. Strict Standardizations: Standardized based on its distinctive hairy stem, trifoliate leaves, and slightly bitter-sweet taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<10%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>12%).

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Used in Karshya (emaciation), Daurbalya (weakness), Vata-vyadhi (neurological disorders), Shukra-dosha (semen anomalies), and Pakshaghata (paralysis).

mahanarayana_taila__medicated_oil_.method: Used in massive classical neuro-oils for systemic massage, paralysis, and arthritis.

kashaya__decoction_.method: Whole plant boiled and reduced. Taken for fever management, rheumatism, and internal bleeding.

seed_powder__churna_.method: 3-6g of fine powder taken daily with warm milk for stamina.

mashaparni_avaleha.method: Special sweet jam made with jaggery to cure extreme muscle wasting.

dosage.children: Generally safe. 1-2 grams of powder mixed with milk for development.
dosage.adults: 3-6g of powder or 30-50ml of decoction per day.
dosage.elderly: Highly recommended to prevent age-related weight loss and maintain physical stability. Dosage: 5g of powder daily.

medicinal_uses: Expert Neuromuscular and Paralysis Specialist (Vata-vyadhi): Mashaparni is the premier Ayurvedic wild creeper for treating paralysis and severe nervous degeneration. Its 'Guru' (heavy), 'Snigdha' (unctuous), and 'Madhura' (sweet) nature helps in providing systemic stabilization of the 'Vata' blockages in the brain and spine.

Reproductive Health and Oligospermia (Shukra-janana): Acts as an elite 'Vajikarana' (aphrodisiac). It deeply nourishes the 'Shukra-dhatu' (reproductive tissue), improving both the quality and quantity of semen.

Physical Strength and Convalescence (Brimhaniya): Historically known as 'Brimhaniya' (nourishing). It effectively improves muscle mass in the extremely emaciated, bedridden, or those recovering from debilitating fevers.
Bone and Hemostatic Specialist: Recognized as 'Raktapitta-hara' (bleeding-destroyer). It effectively cools the blood and stops internal bleeding while providing stability to the joints.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Proteins, Fraxidin, Teramnins, Isoflavonoids, Saponins, and Essential Minerals (Phosphorus, Selenium, Calcium). Isoflavonoids form its anti-oxidative and neuro-protective base.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Extremely high Kapha conditions (severe congestion, heavy indigestion) should avoid large internal doses as it is 'Guru' (heavy).
possible_side_effects: Mild heaviness if digestion is extremely weak.
drug_interactions: None documented. Acts synergistically with nerve tonics.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
